key_finding: K-V DIRECTION DISSOCIATION REPLICATES ACROSS MODELS. Llama K/V ratio = 3.7x (Qwen = 3.6x, nearly identical). V-magnitude immunity: 75/75 combined across both models. KEY DIFFERENCE: Llama is immune to ALL magnitude perturbation (K-mag=100%, 51/51) while Qwen K-mag is devastating (17%). Universal principle: answer computation depends on K-vector DIRECTION (attention routing angles). Magnitude sensitivity is encoding-strategy-specific (digital Qwen vs distributed Llama).
evidence_impact: Strongest cross-model replication in the program. Elevates K-V direction dissociation from model-specific finding to architecture-general mechanism. Refines digital/distributed distinction: both models use K-directions for routing, but only Qwen's digital encoding makes K-magnitudes critical. Opens: K-only PGD, third model family replication, K-mag dose-response on Llama.
next_planned: Priority: (1) K-only PGD to test if null space lives in K-space. (2) K-magnitude dose-response on Llama (find threshold). (3) V-magnitude at higher noise fractions on both models. (4) Third model family replication (Qwen3-8B or Mistral).

null_space_existence.status: strong (Qwen PGD-validated). Mechanism: likely K-direction space

positional_dissociation.status: STRONG: universal across models. Late positions carry answer info via K-routing

geometric_dissociation.status: K-V direction dissociation CROSS-MODEL REPLICATED (3.7x on Llama, 3.6x on Qwen). Magnitude sensitivity encoding-specific

natural_usage.status: H2O/TC protection via position. R-KV shows removing redundant tokens IMPROVES reasoning

cross_model_replication.status: K-V direction dissociation replicated on 2 model families. V-mag immunity 75/75. K-mag differs (digital vs distributed)

encoding_strategy.status: Digital (Qwen: K-mag critical) vs distributed (Llama: K-mag immune). Direction universal. Magnitude encoding-specific

literature_convergence.status: 5+ independent groups converge. K-V dissociation is novel contribution beyond existing literature
